resposta:
solution: 
```class TextEditor:

    def __init__(self):
        self.text = ""
        self.cursor = 0

    def addText(self, text: str) -> None:
        self.text += text
        self.cursor += len(text)

    def deleteText(self, k: int) -> int:
        deleted = min(k, self.cursor)
        self.text = self.text[:self.cursor - deleted] + self.text[self.cursor:]
        self.cursor -= deleted
        return deleted

    def cursorLeft(self, k: int) -> str:
        self.cursor = max(0, self.cursor - k)
        return self.text[:min(10, self.cursor)]

    def cursorRight(self, k: int) -> str:
        self.cursor = min(len(self.text), self.cursor + k)
        return self.text[max(0, self.cursor - 10):self.cursor]
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n + k) where n is the length of the text and k is the number of operations
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: This solution uses a string to store the text and an integer to store the cursor position. The addText method appends the text to the string and updates the cursor position. The deleteText method deletes characters to the left of the cursor by slicing the string and updating the cursor position. The cursorLeft and cursorRight methods update the cursor position and return the last 10 characters to the left of the cursor. The time complexity is O(n + k) because each operation involves slicing the string, which takes O(n) time, and updating the cursor position, which takes O(1) time. The space complexity is O(n) because the string stores the text.
